title: Clematis teuszii
taxonomy: Clematis teuszii is a species of flowering plant in the bignon family Bignoniaceae, but it has been recently reclassified to the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Central Asia, specifically in the Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan regions. The plant is a woody climber with a unique characteristic of having a distinctive shape and fragrance to its flowers. The classification of Clematis teuszii may vary depending on the most recent taxonomic revisions. However, it is widely accepted that it belongs to the subfamily Lamioideae within the family Lamiaceae.

intro: Clematis teuszii, also known as Teusz's clematis, is a climbing plant species native to Cameroon. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Clematis teuszii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Clematis teuszii is used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to boost the immune system.
